Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Zara Okoye's relocation details using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Zara Okoye") or (field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic Robotics"). Agent finds record recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta Status: Completed, Savannah GA → Atlanta GA, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Move dates April 14–16. Special Requirements contain damage claim notes added by Chloe Vance (April 15): cracked servo motor housing on prototype Unit #1, joint calibration misalignment (4+ degrees on 2 axes), prototype valued at $90,000, delivery receipt signed by Kyle Britten (Mosaic intern) NOT Zara Okoye, crating verification failure (Blessing signed off remotely from Philadelphia, anti-vibration foam 4-inch minimum spec not verified, crew lead Marcus Delaney verbal confirmation only).
OE 2: Search Slack for Mosaic prototype damage discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Zara Okoye" or "prototype damage" or "Mosaic damage" or similar). Agent finds in #operations (C006): (1) Emeka Diallo's HIGH-SEVERITY escalation tagging Chloe $90,000 robotic arm with cracked servo motor and misaligned joint calibration; (2) Chloe Vance's compiled incident timeline with Work Order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, Heartland Movers, Blessing's remote sign-off while in Philadelphia; (3) Chloe's final incident summary with financial exposure: $90K damage, $50K MoveOps vendor liability coverage, $40K gap.
OE 3: Search email for Tamara Byrd's complaint using search_emails (query: "Tamara Byrd" or "damaged robotic arm" or "Zara Okoye relocation" or similar). Agent finds email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (CC: rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com), subject "URGENT: Damaged Robotic Arm Prototype -- Zara Okoye Relocation (Savannah to Atlanta)", sent April 15. Tamara demands complete incident report with full chain of custody, due within 48 hours (by EOD April 17). Agent may also find Zara Okoye's internal email to Tamara noting the crating foam around Unit #1 "looked thinner" than Unit #2.
OE 4: Search for Mosaic financial records using search_emails (query: "Hana Kim Mosaic invoice" or "INV-2026-0411" or similar) and quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to "Mosaic" or similar). Agent discovers: Hana Kim's invoice INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 total, includes $1,200 custom crating surcharge pending client approval), credit memo CM-2026-0415 (-$90,000, PENDING adjudication), $50,000 per-item coverage cap, $40,000 gap if Heartland does not accept fault, and liability accrual ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC booked at $90,000 contingent.
OE 5: Identify who signed the delivery receipt instead of Zara Okoye using airtable_search_records (same parameters as OE 1) or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "delivery receipt" or "Kyle Britten" or similar). Agent discovers Kyle Britten (Mosaic Robotics intern) signed instead of Zara Okoye. No damage inspection at delivery (9:15 AM); ~3 hours 45 minutes elapsed before Zara's inspection (~1:00 PM). Found in Airtable damage claim notes or Chloe's Slack messages.
OE 6: Search for Emilia Cruz relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Emilia Cruz"). Agent finds record recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver Status: In Progress, Chicago → Denver, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Move dates April 14–18. Special Requirements mention "Partner's Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs)" and "Potential subcontractor: Gentle Giant Piano Movers (Chicago) NO existing vendor agreement." CRITICAL: Record has never been modified since creation contains no damage notes and still lists Gentle Giant as the potential subcontractor, even though KeyMove Specialty Transport handled the actual piano move.
OE 7: Search for piano damage report using search_emails (query: "Steinway damage" or "Craig Nguyen" or "KeyMove" or "piano extraction" or similar). Agent finds email from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (CC: claims@keymove-specialty.com), subject "Emilia Cruz Steinway damage photos and extraction notes", sent April 11. Reports: Steinway Model B satin ebony, origin 2147 Humboldt St Denver CO. Damage: surface scratch ~4.5–5 inches on bass side rim near keyboard cheek block, finish disruption but no structural crack. Cause: second-floor landing turn tighter than access assessment indicated. 6 photos attached. Crew: Luis Romero and Ben Hall. Agent discovers the ACTUAL vendor was KeyMove Specialty Transport, NOT Gentle Giant listed in Airtable.
OE 8: Search for piano financial handling using search_emails (query: "KeyMove insurance rider" or "Marcus Thorne KeyMove" or similar) and quickbooks_search_vendors (criteria related to "KeyMove" or similar). Agent finds Marcus Thorne email (April 17) confirming KeyMove added $1,200 insurance rider for Emilia, and QuickBooks vendor entry VEND-KEYMOVE-001 (KeyMove Specialty Transport) showing $1,200 balance.
OE 9: Search for Pam Kowalski's NorthWind escalation using search_emails (query: "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind escalation" or "formal escalation NorthWind" or similar). Agent finds email from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC: victor.huang@northwindtech.com), subject "Formal escalation: NorthWind account stability and retention decision", sent April 24. Final warning demanding by end of day Friday: (1) corrective action plan for Raj Patel security incident, (2) resolution path for Emilia Cruz damage claim, (3) named account coverage, (4) commercial consideration. Agent may also discover Victor Huang's security breach email (April 10) about Raj Patel's server lab chain-of-custody violation and prohibited overnight storage. Establishes the NorthWind account is in critical jeopardy.
OE 10: Search for NorthWind retention tracking using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind retention" or "NorthWind escalation" or similar). Agent finds issue linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1: "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" assigned to David Chen, priority 1, labels: northwind, retention-risk, exec-visible. References Victor Huang's breach, Emilia Cruz's damage claim, Julian Brooks support note, and Catalina Dubois's unfulfilled April 14 assurance to Pam.
OE 11: Search for the Road Runner vehicle delay email using search_emails (query: "Road Runner" or "vehicle delay Marcus Webb" or "Honda Civic" or similar). Agent finds email from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com, subject "Delay update: Marcus Webb 2019 Honda Civic shipment to Boston", sent April 11. Vehicle: 2019 Honda Civic LX, blue. Original ETA: April 8. Stuck at Indianapolis transfer hub driver called off, no replacement. Revised window: April 18–20 (tentative). Reference: RR-482991-BOS. Pickup origin: Detroit, MI.
OE 12: Search for the service recovery after Blessing missed the delay email using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb vehicle delay miss" or "Blessing dropped Road Runner" or similar). Agent finds email from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com (CC: julian.brooks@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com), subject "Marcus Webb vehicle delay miss I dropped the April 11 Road Runner notice", sent April 23. Blessing admits she never acted on the April 11 email. Marcus sent two follow-ups that sat unanswered. Julian Brooks picked up the recovery. Gap: ~12 days (Apr 11 → Apr 23). Blessing cites overload: NorthWind turnovers, Mosaic flight changes, Webb equipment coordination.
OE 13: Search for Marcus Webb's Airtable relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb"). Agent finds record recMarcusWebbBrightloop Status: In Progress, Atlanta → Boston, Coordinator: Suki Patel, Move dates April 14–18. Special Requirements mention "2019 Honda Civic (VIN: 2HGFC2F53KH123456), Road Runner Auto Transport required." Note: Coordinator is Suki Patel, but Road Runner's delay email was sent to Blessing, not Suki.
OE 14: Search for BrightLoop ops gap tracking using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop ops gaps" or "Marcus Webb vendor miss" or "BrightLoop audit" or similar). Agent finds issue linear_issue_f85be674c9b8: "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow" assigned to Chloe Vance, priority 2, labels: brightloop, ops-risk, service-recovery, international.
OE 15: Update Zara Okoye's relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta", fields: {...}}]). Agent adds supplementary notes from investigation e.g., Tamara's incident report deadline (EOD April 17), financial details confirmed via QuickBooks. The record already has comprehensive damage documentation from Chloe's April 15 update.
OE 16: Update Emilia Cruz's relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01", records: [{id: "recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver", fields: {...}}]). Agent updates the stale record with: actual vendor (KeyMove Specialty Transport, NOT Gentle Giant), piano damage (scratch ~4.5–5 inches, bass side rim, walkup assessment error), insurance rider ($1,200), 6 damage photos received, and connection to Pam Kowalski's escalation. Critical update record has never been modified since creation and is entirely stale.
OE 17: Create Linear ticket for crating verification gap using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations" or similar, title: "Crating verification process gap remote sign-off failure on Heartland packing checklist" or similar, description: "..."). Tracks the systemic gap exposed by Blessing's remote sign-off without verifying foam density leading to $90,000 prototype damage. Should reference work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE and the 4-inch minimum anti-vibration foam spec.
OE 18: Email Emeka Diallo about Mosaic damage findings using send_email (sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: "Mosaic prototype damage investigation findings" or similar, content: "..."). Summary covers: damage details ($90K, cracked servo housing, joint misalignment), insurance gap ($40K), crating verification failure, delivery receipt issue (Kyle Britten/intern), QuickBooks entries (CM-2026-0415), and Tamara's deadline (April 17).
OE 19: Email Catalina Dubois about NorthWind piano findings using send_email (sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients: ["catalina.dubois@moveops.com"], subject: "NorthWind piano damage KeyMove findings" or similar, content: "..."). Summary covers: Craig Nguyen/KeyMove damage report (scratch, walkup error, 6 photos), insurance rider ($1,200), Airtable record not yet updated, and Pam Kowalski's final warning / NorthWind account crisis context.
OE 20: Post full damage assessment in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: "..."). Comprehensive summary for Chloe covering all three incidents: (1) Mosaic prototype $90K, $40K gap, crating failure, intern delivery receipt; (2) NorthWind piano KeyMove/Craig Nguyen report, walkup error, Pam's escalation; (3) BrightLoop vehicle delay 12-day gap, Julian's recovery. Consolidated so Chloe can brief Elena.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Zara Okoye's relocation details using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Zara Okoye") or (field_name: "Company", value: "Mosaic Robotics"). OE 2: Search Slack for Mosaic prototype damage discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Zara Okoye" or "prototype damage" or "Mosaic damage" or similar). Agent finds in #operations (C006): (1) Emeka Diallo's HIGH-SEVERITY escalation tagging Chloe $90,000 robotic arm with cracked servo motor and misaligned joint calibration; (2) Chloe Vance's compiled incident timeline with Work Order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, Heartland Movers, Blessing's remote sign-off while in Philadelphia; (3) Chloe's final incident summary with financial exposure: $90K damage, $50K MoveOps vendor liability coverage, $40K gap.
OE 3: Search email for Tamara Byrd's complaint using search_emails (query: "Tamara Byrd" or "damaged robotic arm" or "Zara Okoye relocation" or similar). Agent finds email from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (CC: rafael.mendes@mosaicrobotics.com), subject "URGENT: Damaged Robotic Arm Prototype -- Zara Okoye Relocation (Savannah to Atlanta)", sent April 15. Tamara demands complete incident report with full chain of custody, due within 48 hours (by EOD April 17). Agent may also find Zara Okoye's internal email to Tamara noting the crating foam around Unit #1 "looked thinner" than Unit #2.
OE 4: Search for Mosaic financial records using search_emails (query: "Hana Kim Mosaic invoice" or "INV-2026-0411" or similar) and quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to "Mosaic" or similar). Agent discovers: Hana Kim's invoice INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 total, includes $1,200 custom crating surcharge pending client approval), credit memo CM-2026-0415 (-$90,000, PENDING adjudication), $50,000 per-item coverage cap, $40,000 gap if Heartland does not accept fault, and liability accrual ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC booked at $90,000 contingent.
OE 5: Identify who signed the delivery receipt instead of Zara Okoye using airtable_search_records (same parameters as OE 1) or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "delivery receipt" or "Kyle Britten" or similar). Agent discovers Kyle Britten (Mosaic Robotics intern) signed instead of Zara Okoye. No damage inspection at delivery (9:15 AM); ~3 hours 45 minutes elapsed before Zara's inspection (~1:00 PM). OE 18: Email Emeka Diallo about Mosaic damage findings using send_email (sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients: ["emeka.diallo@moveops.com"], subject: "Mosaic prototype damage investigation findings" or similar, content: "..."). Summary covers: damage details ($90K, cracked servo housing, joint misalignment), insurance gap ($40K), crating verification failure, delivery receipt issue (Kyle Britten/intern), QuickBooks entries (CM-2026-0415), and Tamara's deadline (April 17).
OE 19: Email Catalina Dubois about NorthWind piano findings using send_email (sender: "blessing.okafor@moveops.com", recipients: ["catalina.dubois@moveops.com"], subject: "NorthWind piano damage KeyMove findings" or similar, content: "..."). Summary covers: Craig Nguyen/KeyMove damage report (scratch, walkup error, 6 photos), insurance rider ($1,200), Airtable record not yet updated, and Pam Kowalski's final warning / NorthWind account crisis context.
OE 20: Post full damage assessment in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: "..."). Comprehensive summary for Chloe covering all three incidents: (1) Mosaic prototype $90K, $40K gap, crating failure, intern delivery receipt; (2) NorthWind piano KeyMove/Craig Nguyen report, walkup error, Pam's escalation; (3) BrightLoop vehicle delay 12-day gap, Julian's recovery. Consolidated so Chloe can brief Elena.
